name: Aaron Bell
abstract: Samuel Aaron Bell (April 24, 1921 – July 28, 2003) was an American jazz double-bassist.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Ellington's Black, Brown and Beige

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Thompson
about_quotes.evidence: They were done with Skeeter Best, a very, very fine guitarist and Aaron Bell, who had played with Duke Ellington bass, and myself on piano.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Then another time I did some things with playing piano with Johnny and Lawrence, with Aaron Bell on bass and Sonny Greer on drums.

about_quotes.speaker: George Russell
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee does not mention Aaron Bell; the name is only brought up by the interviewer in the context of Jimmy Blanton's hiring timeline.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Russell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: After a time Greer didn't want to travel so we used Sam Woodyard (PHONETIC) on drums and Aaron Bell (PHONETIC) on bass. It was a good band.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Aaron Bell? [INTERVIEWEE] No, no, it wasn't him. He's been around. Good bass player. He still works around.

about_quotes.speaker: Jay McShann
about_quotes.evidence: I used to cut Aaron's hair in school... I'd always get him and I'd cut his hair and he'd just be tickling, laughing
